steps.name: C1 — Inline Conditional Text
steps.criteria: Given the C1 fixture compiled from slice-c1-inline-conditionals.ink.json, when choice 0 is selected (condition false), then 'She's a stranger.' appears in output and 'You know her.' does not appear. When choice 1 is selected (condition true), then 'You know her.' appears and 'She's a stranger.' does not appear. Both selections survive save/restore round-trip. Oracle tests match JS bridge output.

steps.name: C2 — Block and Switch Conditionals
steps.criteria: Given the C2 fixture from slice-c2-block-conditionals.ink.json, choices 0 and 1 exercise if/else branches producing 'You failed.' and 'You passed.' respectively. Choices 2, 3, and 4 exercise switch-style dispatch producing 'Arrested.', 'Escaped.', and 'Unknown.' respectively. All five if/else and switch selections survive save/restore round-trip. Oracle output matches JS bridge for all 5 choices.
steps.scenario_name: Slice C2 — Block and Switch Conditionals

steps.name: C3 — Ink Functions
steps.criteria: Given the C3 fixture from slice-c3-functions.ink.json, choice 0 produces 'The result is 10.' (inline return value from function call). Choice 1 produces 'Stored: 14.' (return value assigned to temp variable). Choice 2 produces 'Done.' with no 'void' literal in output (void suppression via 'out' control command). All three cases survive save/restore round-trip. Oracle output matches JS bridge.

steps.name: T1 — Single-Level Tunnels
steps.criteria: Given the T1 fixture from slice-t1-tunnels.ink.json, three successive continue() calls produce 'Before tunnel.', 'Sub room content.', and 'After tunnel.' in that order. canContinue remains true after the tunnel body and becomes false only after 'After tunnel.' is consumed. Full sequence is correct when the story is rebuilt from save/restore at each continuation step. Oracle output matches JS bridge.

steps.name: T2 — Nested Tunnels
steps.criteria: Given the T2 fixture from slice-t2-nested-tunnels.ink.json, five successive continue() calls produce 'Caller pre.', 'A pre.', 'B body.', 'A post.', and 'Caller post.' in that exact order. 'A post.' appears before 'Caller post.' confirming the outer return address is preserved across nested tunnel entry. Full sequence is correct when the story is rebuilt from save/restore at each continuation step. Oracle output matches JS bridge.

steps.name: T3 — Reference Parameters
steps.criteria: Given the T3 fixture from slice-t3-ref-params.ink.json, calling add(ref score, 10) mutates the global variable 'score' from 0 to 10. Output contains 'Score is 10.' and does NOT contain 'Score is 0.'. The mutated value of 'score' survives save/restore round-trip (restored state reflects the updated value). Oracle output matches JS bridge.
